name: madeineurope.dev
description: A curated directory of developer tools, software, services, and hardware made in Europe.

categories.name: Developer Tools
categories.description: IDEs, CLIs, automation platforms, design-to-code tools, game engines, and productivity tools built or governed in Europe.

entries.name: n8n
entries.description: Workflow automation platform for APIs, SaaS, internal tools, and AI workflows.

origin.evidence: Official imprint lists n8n GmbH in Berlin, Germany.

curation.notes: Top-ranked European project in this research pass by GitHub stars and same-day push activity.

entries.name: Godot Engine
entries.description: Open-source 2D and 3D game engine for games, simulations, and interactive software.

origin.evidence: Godot governance lists the Godot Foundation as a Dutch non-profit.

curation.notes: European foundation governance with public technical governance and recent repository activity.

entries.name: JetBrains IntelliJ Platform
entries.description: IDE platform behind IntelliJ IDEA and many JetBrains developer tools.

origin.evidence: JetBrains publishes Czech Republic office and company contact details.

curation.notes: Canonical community repository is active; commercial JetBrains products use mixed licensing.

entries.name: Penpot
entries.description: Open-source design and prototyping tool built for design and code collaboration.

origin.evidence: Penpot is developed by Kaleidos, a Spanish company, and maintains an open-source repository.

curation.notes: Included as a European-origin developer collaboration tool; verify current legal entity before using for strict sovereignty procurement.

categories.name: Cloud & Hosting
categories.description: European cloud providers, VPS platforms, managed hosting, object storage, and infrastructure services.

entries.name: Hetzner Cloud
entries.description: German cloud provider offering VPS, dedicated servers, object storage, load balancers, and managed infrastructure.

origin.evidence: Hetzner is a German hosting company with European datacenter regions.

curation.notes: Strong fit for developers who need European infrastructure rather than open-source software.

entries.name: OVHcloud
entries.description: French cloud provider offering public cloud, private cloud, bare metal, storage, networking, and managed services.

origin.evidence: OVHcloud is a French cloud provider with European infrastructure and public cloud services.

curation.notes: Useful anchor provider for European cloud procurement.

entries.name: Scaleway
entries.description: French cloud provider with compute, Kubernetes, serverless, storage, database, and AI infrastructure products.

origin.evidence: Scaleway is a European cloud provider operated from France.

curation.notes: Developer-friendly European cloud with broad platform coverage.

entries.name: IONOS Cloud
entries.description: European cloud platform for compute, storage, networking, managed Kubernetes, and enterprise hosting.

origin.evidence: IONOS is a German cloud and hosting provider.

curation.notes: Good candidate for teams comparing European cloud providers.

entries.name: UpCloud
entries.description: European cloud hosting provider with compute, managed databases, networking, and storage.

origin.evidence: UpCloud is a Finnish cloud provider with European origins.

curation.notes: Include as a European cloud option; verify region and compliance needs per workload.

categories.name: Security & Auth
categories.description: European identity, authentication, secrets, password, VPN, and hardware security tools.
categoryUseCases: choose European VPN or security hardware

entries.name: ZITADEL
entries.description: Open-source identity infrastructure for login, SSO, multi-tenancy, and machine-to-machine auth.

origin.evidence: ZITADEL is operated by a Swiss company and publishes a canonical open-source repository.

curation.notes: Good fit for European identity; refresh GitHub metadata in a future automated pass.

entries.name: Hanko
entries.description: Passkey-first authentication and user management for modern web applications.

origin.evidence: Hanko is a German passkey/authentication vendor.

curation.notes: Included for passkey-first auth; verify current license per component.

entries.name: Passbolt
entries.description: Open-source password manager built for teams, with self-hosting and collaboration workflows.

origin.evidence: Passbolt is a European company headquartered in Luxembourg.

curation.notes: Useful for European team credential management.

entries.name: Proton VPN
entries.description: Privacy-focused VPN service from Proton with open-source clients.

origin.evidence: Proton is a Swiss privacy company.

curation.notes: Service is commercial; open-source status applies mainly to client software.

entries.name: Nitrokey
entries.description: Open hardware security keys, encrypted storage, and secure devices for authentication and secrets.

origin.evidence: Nitrokey is a German open hardware security company.

curation.notes: Also appears in Hardware & IoT because it is both a security and hardware vendor.

categories.name: Analytics & Monitoring
categories.description: Privacy analytics, observability, metrics, dashboards, alerting, and production monitoring tools from Europe.

entries.name: Grafana
entries.description: Open observability and data visualization platform for metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerts.

origin.evidence: data.europa.eu identifies Grafana Labs as a Sweden use case and describes Grafana's Swedish origin.

curation.notes: Top-five European project in this research pass.

entries.name: Matomo
entries.description: Open-source web analytics platform focused on data ownership and privacy.

origin.evidence: Matomo has strong European adoption and privacy positioning but should be verified for strict European-only procurement.

curation.notes: Strong privacy/open-source fit, but European origin confidence is lower than Europe-registered entries.

entries.name: Plausible Analytics
entries.description: Lightweight, privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics with cloud and self-hosting options.

origin.evidence: Plausible is commonly presented as an Estonia-based privacy analytics company.

curation.notes: Good developer-friendly analytics choice; verify legal entity details when used for strict procurement.

entries.name: Netdata
entries.description: Real-time infrastructure monitoring and troubleshooting with per-second metrics.

origin.evidence: Netdata was founded by Greek engineer Costa Tsaousis, but current legal materials identify Netdata, Inc. in Delaware with a San Francisco principal place of business.

curation.notes: Highly starred and maintained, but not treated as Europe-primary in top-five ranking because current legal status is US-based.

categories.name: Hardware & IoT
categories.description: European hardware, IoT software, open hardware, smart-home platforms, boards, keys, and maker tooling.

entries.name: Home Assistant
entries.description: Open-source home automation platform focused on local control and privacy.

origin.evidence: Open Home Foundation owns and protects Home Assistant and lists Swiss registration identifier CHE-416.988.952.

curation.notes: Top-five European project in this research pass.

entries.name: Arduino
entries.description: Open hardware and software ecosystem for microcontroller prototyping and education.

origin.evidence: Arduino is an Italian-origin hardware and software ecosystem.

curation.notes: Arduino IDE 1.x repository is less active than the broader Arduino ecosystem; include newer Arduino repos in future passes.

entries.name: PrusaSlicer
entries.description: Open-source slicer for 3D printing workflows, maintained by Prusa Research.

origin.evidence: Prusa Research is a Czech 3D-printing hardware company.

curation.notes: Strong European hardware-adjacent developer tool.

entries.name: KiCad
entries.description: Open-source electronics design automation suite for schematic capture and PCB layout.

origin.evidence: KiCad has European origins and significant European institutional support, including CERN contributions.

curation.notes: Canonical source is GitLab rather than GitHub; include for hardware developers despite not fitting GitHub-star ranking.

entries.name: Nitrokey
entries.description: Open hardware security keys, encrypted storage, and secure computing devices.

origin.evidence: Nitrokey is a German open hardware security company.

curation.notes: Cross-listed with Security & Auth because its primary value is hardware-backed security.

categories.name: Databases & Storage
categories.description: European search, vector databases, collaboration storage, object storage, and data infrastructure.
categoryUseCases: run vector search for AI apps

entries.name: Elasticsearch
entries.description: Distributed search and analytics engine used for search, observability, security, and retrieval workloads.

origin.evidence: Elastic N.V. is registered with a legal address in Amsterdam, Netherlands.

curation.notes: Top-five European project in this research pass; license should be reviewed per deployment.

entries.name: Qdrant
entries.description: High-performance vector database and vector search engine for AI applications.

origin.evidence: Qdrant is operated by Qdrant Solutions GmbH in Berlin, Germany.

curation.notes: Strong European AI/data infrastructure candidate with same-day repository activity.

entries.name: Nextcloud
entries.description: Self-hosted collaboration and file sync platform for documents, storage, sharing, and apps.

origin.evidence: Nextcloud GmbH is a German company behind the Nextcloud server project.

curation.notes: Good European storage/collaboration default for self-hosting needs.

entries.name: CrateDB
entries.description: Distributed SQL database for real-time analytics, time-series, and machine data.

origin.evidence: CrateDB has European company roots and operations.

curation.notes: Include as a European database candidate; refresh GitHub metadata in a future pass.

entries.name: SurrealDB
entries.description: Multi-model database for modern applications with SQL-like querying and real-time APIs.

origin.evidence: SurrealDB is a UK-origin database company and open-source project.

curation.notes: Promising European-origin database; license is source-available rather than OSI open source.

categories.name: Communication & Email
categories.description: European email, messaging, team communication, transactional mail, and notification tools.

entries.name: Element
entries.description: Secure collaboration client built on the open Matrix protocol.

origin.evidence: Element is a UK company and core contributor to the Matrix ecosystem.

curation.notes: Useful for developers who need federated and self-hostable communication.

entries.name: Proton Mail
entries.description: Encrypted email service and privacy suite from Switzerland.

origin.evidence: Proton is a Swiss privacy company.

curation.notes: Commercial service with open-source client components.

entries.name: Tuta
entries.description: Encrypted email, calendar, and contacts service from Germany.

origin.evidence: Tuta is operated by Tutao GmbH in Germany.

curation.notes: Strong European encrypted mail option.

entries.name: Mailjet
entries.description: Email delivery and marketing platform with APIs for transactional email.

origin.evidence: Mailjet was founded in France and is a European email platform.

curation.notes: Good service option; verify current legal/controller details for strict procurement.

entries.name: Brevo
entries.description: Customer communication platform for email, SMS, marketing automation, and transactional messaging.

origin.evidence: Brevo is a French customer communication platform.

curation.notes: Commercial European communications option.

categories.name: AI & ML
categories.description: European AI APIs, model providers, vector search, ML infrastructure, translation, and AI application tooling.
categoryUseCases: build AI products with European providers

entries.name: Mistral AI
entries.description: French AI company providing open-weight and commercial language models plus hosted APIs.

origin.evidence: Mistral AI is a French AI company.

curation.notes: Important European AI provider; model licenses vary by release.

entries.name: Qdrant
entries.description: Vector database and semantic search engine for AI applications.

origin.evidence: Qdrant is operated by Qdrant Solutions GmbH in Berlin, Germany.

curation.notes: Cross-listed with Databases & Storage because it is both AI infrastructure and database infrastructure.

entries.name: DeepL
entries.description: AI translation and writing platform with developer APIs.

origin.evidence: DeepL is a German AI translation company.

curation.notes: Commercial API rather than open-source project.

entries.name: Aleph Alpha
entries.description: German AI company focused on sovereign AI, enterprise AI, and public-sector use cases.

origin.evidence: Aleph Alpha is a German AI company.

curation.notes: Relevant for sovereignty-oriented AI procurement.

entries.name: Jina AI
entries.description: AI search company providing embedding, reranking, reader, and neural search tooling.

origin.evidence: Jina AI has Berlin roots and European AI/search infrastructure presence.

curation.notes: Included as a European-origin AI tooling company; verify current corporate structure in strict procurement.

categories.name: Payments & Billing
categories.description: European payment processors, billing platforms, invoicing tools, SEPA, iDEAL, and PSD2-ready services.

entries.name: Adyen
entries.description: Dutch payments platform for online, in-person, marketplace, and global payment processing.

origin.evidence: Adyen is a Dutch payments company headquartered in Amsterdam.

curation.notes: Enterprise-grade European payment processor.

entries.name: Mollie
entries.description: Dutch payment service provider with APIs and support for European payment methods.

origin.evidence: Mollie is a Dutch payment service provider.

curation.notes: Developer-friendly European PSP for startups and SMBs.

entries.name: GoCardless
entries.description: Bank payment network for direct debit, recurring payments, and account-to-account payments.

origin.evidence: GoCardless is a UK payments company.

curation.notes: Strong fit for recurring bank payments and SEPA collection.

entries.name: Lago
entries.description: Open-source metering and billing platform for usage-based pricing.

origin.evidence: Lago is a French open-source billing company.

curation.notes: Good European open-source billing option for SaaS teams.

entries.name: SumUp
entries.description: European payments company offering card readers, online payments, invoicing, and merchant tools.

origin.evidence: SumUp has European roots and major operations in London and Berlin.

curation.notes: Useful for hardware-assisted payments and merchant tooling; verify API fit per project.
